loops.finding: Fleet stop directives needed a machine-readable provider inventory ACK before a pod could be treated as owned or idle.
loops.implementation: Added a public-safe parser for owner, provider, pod_id, status, job, stop_condition, no-start/no-compute ACK, and no_submit_ack.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Accept complete provider inventory ACKs without opening compute.
bluesky.best_case: Storm can distinguish a stopped owned pod from stale or prose-only watcher text.
bluesky.missing_measurement: A deterministic pass line for complete inventory packets.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Validate one stopped no-compute inventory ACK fixture.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Hold inventory if any ownership or provider field is missing.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: A stopped pod with no job is useful only as control-plane inventory.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if the packet asks to launch, restart, submit, alert, or run compute.

redsky.route_or_claim: A watcher ACK can prove pod ownership and idle state.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no candidate or compute unlock is active
redsky.strongest_objection: A prose ACK can omit provider status or job state and still sound actionable.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Require the pass fixture to emit inventory-ack-accepted-no-compute.

loops.finding: Unknown or unreachable provider inventory must not be accepted as a clean idle pod.
loops.implementation: Added a hold fixture for owner=UNKNOWN with status=unreachable and provider_inventory_not_verified.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Keep unreachable inventory visible but non-actionable.
bluesky.best_case: Workers can report uncertainty without forcing Storm to treat it as a fail or pass.
bluesky.missing_measurement: A deterministic hold line with provider_inventory_not_verified.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Validate one unreachable inventory fixture under --require-pass.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Ask for provider-dashboard refresh rather than pod action.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: Unreachable inventory can still carry useful next-step metadata.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if the packet lacks an owner, provider, pod_id, status, job, or stop condition.

redsky.route_or_claim: Unreachable inventory can be treated as harmless because no compute is requested.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no candidate or compute unlock is active
redsky.strongest_objection: Unknown provider state hides stale spend, orphaned jobs, or inaccessible pods.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Require --require-pass to reject the hold fixture.

loops.finding: Ownerless running pods and start language need a fail decision, not a hold.
loops.implementation: Added fail checks for ownerless running pods, missing stop condition, premature compute/start request, and submit/alert language.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Fail unsafe inventory packets sharply so cleanup work can be routed.
bluesky.best_case: Ownerless running pods are surfaced before spending or duplicate scanners continue.
bluesky.missing_measurement: Failure reasons include ownerless_running_pod and premature_compute_or_start_request.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Validate one ownerless running fixture that also asks to start compute.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: A worker can repost with owner, stop condition, and no-start ACK.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: A fail packet is for cleanup, not for new compute dispatch.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if the packet includes launch, start, restart, scale, dispatch, benchmark, eval, submit, alert, or winner language.

redsky.route_or_claim: A running ownerless pod can be sorted out after the next route packet.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no candidate or compute unlock is active
redsky.strongest_objection: Waiting turns orphaned compute into spend and stale evidence.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Require the fail fixture to emit ownerless_running_pod and premature_compute_or_start_request.

loops.finding: The same heat-stop audit missed Mac-local proof and scanner loops beyond build_circuit and eval_circuit.
loops.implementation: Expanded local-heavy matching to storm-exact-miner, route_compare, search_driver, lower-q, source-line, FFG lifetime/proof, proof-loop, and scanner terms.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Make MacBook heat-stop enforcement cover the actual ECDSA proof/search vocabulary.
bluesky.best_case: Local process snapshots catch proof loops before they burn laptop resources or create stale claims.
bluesky.missing_measurement: The fail fixture reports seven local-heavy lines and local_recurring=1.
bluesky.smallest_useful_experiment: Run the hardened local-heavy fail fixture.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Allow remote-owned heavy commands only when owner and route metadata are present.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: The vocabulary list should stay conservative and source-bound.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if benign one-shot control-plane checks are incorrectly blocked in pass fixtures.

redsky.route_or_claim: Existing local-heavy patterns already catch the dangerous work.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no local official run or compute unlock is active
redsky.strongest_objection: Workers were using storm-exact-miner, route_compare, search_driver, proof, and scanner wording that the gate did not name.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Run the local-heavy fail fixture and check for storm-exact-miner.py and route_compare in terms.

loops.finding: The new process control needed to be wired into the public harness, not left as a standalone helper.
loops.implementation: Added required files, text checks, fixture checks, and this five-loop JSON audit packet to the public harness check.

bluesky.route_or_idea: Make the process improvement durable in CI-style local checks.
bluesky.best_case: Future edits that weaken pod inventory or Mac heat-stop checks fail immediately.
bluesky.missing_measurement: Full public harness pass after the new gate and audit packet are added.
bluesky.bounded_alternative: Keep the gate manual but require every worker to remember it.
bluesky.hidden_assumption: The public harness check remains fast enough for every process edit.
bluesky.stop_condition: Stop if the harness check needs private provider state or live ECDSA compute.

redsky.route_or_claim: A standalone process gate is enough once documented.
redsky.current_public_truth_checked: frontier d44cad3/q1152/1571592960; no candidate or submit language included
redsky.strongest_objection: Standalone helpers rot unless the repo-level check runs them.
redsky.fastest_falsifier: Run the public harness check and the loop gate on this packet.
